page: Microsoft Azure FX8-2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core supporting 2 threads, resulting in 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with an exceptionally high memory capacity of 168.0 GB, yielding 84.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The high clock speed of the Intel Xeon processor drives top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2348.0 and strong single-threaded compression and decompression results, such as 34.74 GB/sec in gzip. However, due to the low core count of 1 physical core, multi-core performance is limited, with Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score is average, the large-block uncached read memory bandwidth is poor at 21.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8-2ms_v2 offers an extreme memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it highly specialized. It provides a dense memory footprint without requiring the acquisition of unnecessary CPU cores, which is advantageous for memory-bound workloads that do not scale horizontally or require massive parallel processing. This profile is suited for memory-intensive applications with low parallel processing demands, such as small-scale in-memory databases, single-threaded data processing tasks, and memory-heavy caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs operating at 4.2 GHz, paired with an exceptionally large 168.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2348.0 and strong single-threaded compression performance. Conversely, multi-core and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks are weak due to the low core count. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a massive memory footprint without high parallel processing requirements. It is best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications, small in-memory databases, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 168 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive VM with 2 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high memory-to-core ratio virtual machine featuring a fast clock speed for memory-intensive, single-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Extremely high memory allocation of 168.0 GB RAM with 2 vCPUs
Top-tier single-core performance and strong single-threaded compression
No local storage or bundled GPUs, with premium storage support
Suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and caching
